markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

The open seat vacated by Rep. Wesley Hunt's U.S. Senate bid has drawn robust GOP fundraising, led by Trump-endorsed mortgage banker Jon Bonck ($1.48 million raised) over West Houston Airport President Shelly deZevallos ahead of their May 26 primary runoff. McDonough trails significantly at $48,000 raised, underscoring limited Democratic path in this Solid Republican seat per Cook Political Report, with no general election polls shifting sentiment.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: The House of Elders (Guurti) voted on April 29, 2026, to extend House of Representatives and local council mandates by 27 months to September 2028—far exceeding the National Electoral Commission's February request for a 10-month delay citing security concerns, biometric voter registration challenges, and drought—driving trader consensus to 63% for no parliamentary election before 2027 amid Somaliland's history of repeated postponements since the last vote in 2021. Waddani holds 30% implied probability as the leading party, bolstered by President Abdirahman Irro's decisive 64% presidential win in November 2024 and its slight 2021 parliamentary edge (31 seats to Kulmiye's 30), while UCID trails at 5% and former ruling Kulmiye at 2% reflects diminished momentum. President Irro has 21 days to promulgate the extension or it auto-approves, with potential legal challenges looming.

markets.question: Will Petr Yan be the next fighter to be ranked first in the UFC Pound-For-Pound Rankings in 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Ilia Topuria at 76.5% implied probability to claim UFC pound-for-pound #1 status in 2026, reflecting his ascent to #2 following a dominant second-round KO of Charles Oliveira to capture the lightweight title at UFC 317 last June, extending his undefeated 17-0 record as a two-division champion after dethroning featherweight kingpin Alexander Volkanovski. Islam Makhachev retains the top spot as welterweight champion per the May 5 rankings update post-UFC Fight Night: Della Maddalena vs. opponent, bolstering the 37.5% "Islam/None" outcome amid his title defenses. Recent UFC 328 upsets like Sean Strickland's have minimally shifted top hierarchies, underscoring Topuria's stylistic edge and activity as key drivers.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors incumbent President Iliana Iotova at 58% for Bulgaria's presidential election by autumn 2026, driven by former President Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria securing an absolute parliamentary majority on April 19 with 44.6% of the vote and 130 seats. As Radev's former vice president now serving in the role, Iotova benefits from this demonstrated voter support amid years of coalition instability, reinforced by her May 5 granting of the first government-formation mandate to Radev. Nikolai Denkov and Vassil Terziev, both at 12.5% from centrist PP-DB, trail as their alliances captured just 12.6% in parliament, while GERB's Rosen Zhelyazkov lags further. The direct popular vote may require a runoff if no first-round majority.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 20°C on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Korea Meteorological Administration's latest short-term forecast, issued May 12, projects Seoul's May 14 high temperature in a wide 17–29°C range, reflecting ensemble model spread amid uncertainty in post-frontal clearing after today's expected 5–10 mm rainfall. Trader consensus favors warmer outcomes at 36.2% for 27°C or higher and 26.5% for exactly 26°C, driven by potential high-pressure ridging and subsidence promoting diurnal heating under mostly clear skies from May 13 onward, contrasting recent observed highs around 20°C. Cooler scenarios (24–25°C at 17.5–18%) hinge on lingering clouds or light northerly winds capping temperatures near climatological mid-May norms of 23–25°C. Watch KMA's evening update for refined model runs narrowing the spread.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping visit US before 2027?
markets.description:
If Xi Jinping visits the United States between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Xi Jinping physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the United States. Whether or not Xi Jinping enters US airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the US or China, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 70% implied probability on Chinese President Xi Jinping visiting the United States before 2027, driven by U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated public invitations and expectations for a reciprocal state visit later in 2026, following their prior meetings and Trump's current summit in Beijing on May 13-15—the first by a sitting U.S. president since 2017. Official statements from late 2025 and early 2026, including Trump's pledges for Xi to travel to Washington or other sites by year-end, underscore diplomatic momentum amid discussions on trade, Taiwan, Iran tensions, and economic ties. This reflects the wisdom of crowds betting on stabilized bilateral relations yielding the visit, though uncertainties like escalating conflicts or domestic politics could alter trajectories before resolution on December 31, 2026.

markets.question: Will Trump’s approval rating be at least 40.0 on May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to Silver Bulletin's approval rating for Donald Trump on May 15, 2026.

Note that the approval ratings for this date must be finalized before it is considered for this market (namely, once the next data point is available, the previous one is finalized).

This market's resolution source will be Silver Bulletin's approval rating poll aggregator, https://www.natesilver.net/p/trump-approval-ratings-nate-silver-bulletin, specifically the approval rating indicated by the green trend line for the resolution date. Changes in the methodology by which Silver Bulletin calculates the approval rating will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. If Silver Bulletin's approval rating becomes permanently unavailable, RealClearPolitics will be used.

The resolution source reports the rating value to only one decimal point (e.g., 42.8%, 33.9%, etc.). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Trump's job approval rating on May 15 in the narrow 38.5–39.9% range, reflecting recent national polls averaging 39–41% amid persistent dissatisfaction with the ongoing Iran war and record-high gas prices. Polling aggregates like RealClearPolitics show 40.6% approval through May 10 (Rasmussen at 43%, NPR/Marist at 39%, Economist/YouGov at 40%), down from earlier highs due to 61% of Americans deeming the conflict a mistake and half of Republicans blaming Trump for inflation and energy costs. Declining independent support—down 18 points since May 2025—anchors the closely contested odds, with no major events scheduled before resolution but potential new surveys influencing final positioning.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: United Russia's 95.5% implied probability on Polymarket reflects trader consensus on its entrenched dominance in the upcoming State Duma elections set for September 18–20, 2026, where 450 seats are contested via a parallel system of 225 party-list proportional representation seats and 225 single-member constituencies. Recent April polls from FOM and WCIOM show United Russia leading party-list voting intentions at 28–52%, ahead of New People (6–13%), KPRF (9–11%), and LDPR (10%), but its historical edge in single-member districts—bolstered by administrative mobilization—secures supermajorities, as in 2021's 324 seats from 49.8% list votes. Late April primaries targeted 10–14% turnout via public-sector drives, while opposition parties like LDPR face infighting and legal woes, and Just Russia grapples with leadership losses. Scenarios to upend this include major economic shocks, security incidents, or war-related developments eroding Kremlin control.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump administration criticisms of NATO have escalated following allies' reluctance to back US military actions against Iran, prompting President Trump to publicly question US membership and order the redeployment of 5,000 troops from Germany on May 2 amid a feud with Berlin. However, congressional legislation from the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act mandates 120-day notification and effectively requires Senate approval—potentially a two-thirds vote—for withdrawal, creating formidable institutional barriers beyond NATO's Article 13 one-year notice provision. No formal denunciation process has begun, with trader consensus reflecting these legal hurdles despite rhetorical pressures; watch for outcomes from Trump-Rutte discussions and potential NATO summit adjustments.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket pins Israel’s Noam Bettan (“Michelle”) and Greece’s Akylas (“Ferto”) in a dead heat at 27% implied probabilities each to claim the Eurovision 2026 televote crown, fueled by standout national final victories earlier this year and glowing first-rehearsal reports highlighting their emotional staging and vocal prowess just days ago. Israel leverages diaspora voting muscle and past televote dominance, while Greece rides “Ferto’s” viral backstory of personal triumph and infectious pop hook, edging out Finland’s high-energy duet Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen (“Liekinheitin”) at 13.5% amid its broader frontrunner status for juries. Romania (11.9%) gains from public betting surges, but the top trio’s buzz dominates ahead of today’s Semi-Final 1—featuring Israel and Finland—with the grand final on May 16 poised to swing on live performances and global fan campaigns.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the NY-12 Democratic primary favors Assemblyman Alex Bores at 47% implied probability, ahead of fellow Assemblyman Micah Lasher at 35.5% and Jack Schlossberg at 15.5%, driven by a fierce super PAC ad war over AI regulation that has boosted Bores' name recognition despite $2.4 million in attacks from OpenAI-backed groups. A late April poll showed Bores leading at 38% among informed voters familiar with top contenders, with his support intensifying amid exposure. Lasher benefits from endorsements by retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler, Gov. Kathy Hochul, and Michael Bloomberg's $4 million super PAC. Schlossberg's early poll leads have faded amid low projected youth turnout, as an AARP analysis projects voters 50+ comprising 65-75% of the June 23 electorate in this open Manhattan seat. Recent forums and a May Jewish issues debate highlighted divisions on policy, keeping the race fluid.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's commanding 70% victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26, securing the official endorsement and advancing alongside Brian Shortsleeve while eliminating Mike Kennealy below the 15% delegate threshold, drives his 84.5% implied probability as the GOP gubernatorial primary frontrunner. Minogue's fundraising edge—$9.9 million cash on hand versus Shortsleeve's $732,000—powers statewide TV ads and digital outreach exceeding 15 million impressions, bolstering voter outreach in the low-turnout primary. Shortsleeve holds 16.4% amid calls for debates that Minogue has skipped, but trader consensus reflects the endorsement's weight in Massachusetts GOP dynamics ahead of the September 1 primary.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 72.5% implied probability against the U.S. enacting a comprehensive AI safety bill before 2027, driven by stalled federal legislative progress in the divided 119th Congress. Numerous bills, including H.R. 8037 (Protect American AI Act) introduced in March 2026 and the AI Accountability Act, remain mired in committee without floor votes or hearings. The Trump administration's National AI Legislative Framework, released March 20, 2026, advocates light-touch regulations emphasizing innovation, child protections, and federal preemption of state AI development rules, rather than broad safety mandates. Amid over 600 state AI bills advancing in 2026 sessions, bipartisan federal efforts focus narrowly on issues like AI chatbots for minors, leaving enactment prospects dim before the December 2026 session end.

eventMetadata.context_description: Abdul El-Sayed's trader consensus lead at 49.5% in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary reflects his surge in recent polling, including a May 1-7 Mitchell Research survey showing him at 28% ahead of Haley Stevens (18%) and Mallory McMorrow (17%), driven by strong support among voters under 40 and progressives highlighted in April Emerson and Data for Progress crosstabs. El-Sayed's standing ovation at the late-April Michigan Democratic Party convention and robust Q1 fundraising alongside McMorrow have narrowed Stevens' earlier edges from Glengariff polls, positioning the U.S. representative third at 20.3%. McMorrow's 27.5% stems from favorability with older Democrats, women, and independents, keeping the August 4 primary wide open amid generational divides on issues like Medicare for All.

markets.question: LoL: Frites Esports Club vs mCon esports (BO3) - Road Of Legends Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Frites Esports Club and mCon esports in the Road Of Legends Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 12 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Frites Esports Club" if Frites Esports Club win the match against mCon esports.

This market will resolve to "mCon esports" if mCon esports win the match against Frites Esports Club.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPY) hit (LOW) $730 Week of May 11 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 11 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPY) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the S&P 500 (SPY) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Janet Horner win the 2026 Dublin-central by-election?
markets.description:
A by-election for a seat from the Dublin Central constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026to fill the vacancy left by the resignation of Paschal Donohoe.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Dublin-Central seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Social Democrats councillor Daniel Ennis leads trader consensus at 75.5% implied probability for the Dublin Central Dáil by-election on May 22, buoyed by party TD Gary Gannon's strong 2024 performance in the diverse four-seat constituency and Ennis's North Strand roots plus effective grassroots canvassing on housing, cost-of-living, and community safety. Sinn Féin's Janice Boylan trails at 17.6%, leveraging leader Mary Lou McDonald's local base despite her recent claim that immigration concerns are absent on doorsteps, drawing criticism amid ongoing debates. Independent Gerry Hutch holds 6% as a wildcard with past first-preference appeal, while Fine Gael's Ray McAdam sits at 4.1% facing left-wing dominance. No formal polls exist, but traditional bookmakers align with Polymarket pricing post-May 3 RTÉ debate and nominations closure on May 2; transfers in PR-STV counting remain key amid undecided voters.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Analilia Mejia's 20-25% margin of victory in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, reflecting certified tallies showing her defeating Republican Joe Hathaway 60.2% to 39.4%—a 20.8-point edge—with all precincts reporting. This outcome stems from the Democratic-leaning district's history, where outgoing Rep. Mikie Sherrill (now governor) secured large wins, compounded by Democratic overperformance in recent House special elections and low special-election turnout favoring the progressive nominee backed by figures like Bernie Sanders. Pockets of softer support in Jewish-heavy towns like Livingston highlighted Israel policy divides, but did not dent the landslide. Minimal shifts from any remaining provisional ballots or final canvass pose the only realistic challenge, per historical New Jersey election patterns.

markets.question: Will Nicolae Ciucă be the next Prime Minister of Romania?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Romania by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Nicușor Dan prepares to nominate Romania's next prime minister following the May 5 no-confidence vote that toppled Ilie Bolojan's pro-EU coalition government, now in caretaker mode amid budget deficit pressures and PSD's April withdrawal from the grand coalition. Trader consensus favors PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu at 24.5% implied probability, reflecting the party's status as largest parliamentary force and his prior signals of interest, while PNL's Deputy Prime Minister Cătălin Predoiu trails closely at 16.5% as a continuity pro-EU option. The race remains tight due to fragmented parliament requiring coalition negotiations for a confidence vote; separation could arise from presidential consultations this week, party endorsements, or snap election threats if no majority forms.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Córdoba CF vs. Albacete Balompié: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game between Córdoba CF and Albacete Balompié, scheduled for May 15 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Córdoba CF and Albacete Balompié combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Córdoba CF vs. Albacete Balompié - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 15 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Córdoba CF hosts Albacete Balompié in a mid-table La Liga 2 encounter at Estadio Nuevo Arcángel on May 15, with the 10th-placed Blanquiverdes holding a slight edge via home form and their 3-1 away win over Albacete in October 2025. Both teams sit safely from relegation but eye playoff positioning after 37 matches, Córdoba at 54 points (15-9-13 record) ahead of Albacete's lower tally. Recent form favors Córdoba, who claimed victories over Zaragoza and Cultural Leonesa in April before a narrow loss to Sporting Gijón, while Albacete manages modest away results. Key absences include Córdoba's winger Adilson (ACL tear) and Albacete's forward Higinio Marín (ankle) plus midfielder Antonio Puertas (ACL); monitor official injury reports and lineups for impact on scoring potential. Mild 23°C weather poses no issues.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Denmark's snap general election on March 24, where Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats secured the largest bloc in the Folketing but fell short of a majority, coalition negotiations have dragged on amid a fragmented parliament. Frederiksen, initially tasked by King Frederik X to form a government, conceded failure on May 8, prompting the monarch to appoint Liberal Party leader and Defence Minister Troels Lund Poulsen to explore a centre-right coalition, boosting his trader consensus to 15.3%. Despite this, markets imply 69.5% odds for Frederiksen as next prime minister, driven by her party's seat lead, historical dealmaking prowess in minority governments, and Poulsen's two-week mandate announced May 11 potentially faltering on policy sticking points like defence and immigration. Lars Løkke Rasmussen's 8.9% reflects Moderates' pivotal swing role in coalition talks.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Emmanuel Macron barred by term limits from seeking a third consecutive presidency, the 2027 French presidential election—set for April with a potential second round two weeks later—features a fragmented field requiring 500 elected endorsements from at least 30 departments to appear on the ballot. Recent polls from early May show Rassemblement National's Jordan Bardella leading first-round voting intentions at around 30%, buoyed by post-municipal election momentum, while former Prime Minister Édouard Philippe polls strongly as the top centrist-right contender against the far right. Jean-Luc Mélenchon formalized his fourth candidacy on May 3 amid left-wing primary debates, and Les Républicains nominated Bruno Retailleau. Traders eye upcoming party primaries, further declarations, and polling shifts in this open race.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 81.5% implied probability for a Bank of Russia key rate decrease at the June 19 meeting, driven by sustained disinflation and the ongoing easing cycle. markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (HIGH) $140 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or above the listed price between market creation and the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant Lower bracket semifinal match between T1 and Paper Rex in the VCT Pacific Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 15 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "T1" if T1 win the match against Paper Rex.

This market will resolve to "Paper Rex" if Paper Rex win the match against T1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: With ballots now mailing for the June 16, 2026, Democratic primary—the District's first using ranked-choice voting—traders view Ward 4 Councilmember Janeese Lewis George as the clear frontrunner at 66.5% implied probability, driven by her record grassroots fundraising exceeding 5,700 local donors, recent $133,000 haul from over 2,100 contributors, and endorsements from former Attorney General Karl Racine and at-large Councilmember Robert White in the open contest after incumbent Mayor Muriel Bowser opted against a fourth term. Former Councilmember Kenyan McDuffie holds second at 23%, gaining from ex-Mayor Anthony Williams's May 7 backing and centrist appeals amid April debates clashing over crime surges, affordability, and experience. Fresh fact-checks rebut attacks on Lewis George's policing positions and McDuffie's sports betting record, as candidates also address President Trump's policy shadow ahead of early voting.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Pulitzer" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

markets.question: Google (GOOGL) Up or Down on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on May 12, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on May 12, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 39.8% implied probability for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation falling in the 30.0-34.9% range, closely tracking the central bank's latest REM survey median forecast of 30.5%—up 1.4 percentage points amid March 2026's 3.4% monthly CPI acceleration to 32.6% year-over-year, the highest print of the year driven by education and transportation costs. President Milei's fiscal austerity and monetary tightening have sustained disinflation from 2025 peaks above 40%, though regulated price adjustments and wage pressures cap further declines, positioning nearby bins at 25-27% probabilities. April monthly CPI data, expected around 2.6%, looms as the next key catalyst for year-end projections.

markets.question: Ukraine agrees not to join NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ukraine publicly agrees not to join NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Ukraine not to join NATO will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the Russian Federation.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO for any amount of time will count (e.g. If Ukraine not to join NATO for 10 years this will qualify).

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995 “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement is an example of a qualifying agreement. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the Ukraine, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming a qualifying agreement has been reached will also count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 79% on Ukraine agreeing not to pursue NATO membership before 2027, reflecting stalled Russia-Ukraine peace talks initiated under U.S. mediation in late 2025. President Zelenskyy floated dropping NATO ambitions in December 2025 for Western security guarantees amid Geneva negotiations, but rejected renouncing aspirations per a proposed 28-point plan, insisting on retaining alliance options. February 2026 trilateral talks emphasized military de-escalation and territorial issues without NATO concessions, as ongoing Russian advances and NATO's lack of membership consensus—highlighted by Secretary General Rutte—persist. With no breakthroughs in the past 30 days and a July 2026 NATO summit in Türkiye unlikely to alter dynamics, traders see slim odds of a formal pre-2027 pledge.

markets.question: Will Applied Materials' Semiconductor Systems revenue be above $5.9B in Q2?

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market.

If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

Espanyol's trader sentiment is weighed down by a dismal winless streak spanning all of 2026—last victory a 2-1 upset at San Mamés against Athletic on December 22, 2025—managing just six points from 48 possible amid a grueling run-in facing Real Madrid, Sevilla, Osasuna, and Real Sociedad. Athletic, sitting slightly higher around 10th-12th with 38-41 points after 33 matches, boasts steadier recent form but grapples with lingering concerns over Nico Williams' groin recovery. Head-to-head favors the hosts lately, yet Espanyol's home form and desperation clash against Athletic's away resilience, with no major injuries reported in the past week.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Uncertainty has lifted Justin Fields to 42.5% following his April trade from the Jets for a 2027 sixth-rounder, positioning the mobile backup as prime insurance eager to absorb Andy Reid's scheme. Gardner Minshew (42.2%), Joe Flacco (41.9%), and practice squad standout Chris Oladokun (37%) reflect depth chart viability amid cap restructures and seventh-round draft pick Garrett Nussmeier, highlighting real Week 1 rotation risks in Kansas City's veteran-laden QB room.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures have surged above $101 per barrel as of May 12, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on heightened geopolitical risks, particularly Strait of Hormuz tensions amid stalled US-Iran ceasefire talks and threats of prolonged disruptions. June 2026 contracts trade near $99.50, implying modest backwardation amid supply concerns offsetting softer global demand growth projected by EIA at 650 kb/d for the year. OPEC+ production cuts, extended through 2026 with a planned 188 kb/d unwind in June, provide floor support, while recent EIA inventories for the week ending May 1 showed modest builds. Key catalysts ahead include the June 7 OPEC+ JMMC review and weekly inventory reports, which could sway market-implied odds toward resolution by month-end.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat April McClain-Delaney holds a commanding position in Maryland's 6th Congressional District ahead of the June 23 primaries, driving trader consensus to an 87.5% implied probability for the Democratic Party nominee in the November general election. Recent race ratings from Cook Political Report (Solid Democratic as of May 5), Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball reinforce this view, citing her narrow 2024 victory, strong endorsements from Gov. Wes Moore, Sen. Chris Van Hollen, and Rep. Nancy Pelosi, and massive fundraising edge—over $3 million cash on hand versus Republicans' under $25,000 combined. A fragmented GOP primary featuring low-funded candidates like Robin Ficker and Christopher Burnett offers little threat in this D-leaning battleground district, despite its western Maryland conservative tilt. Escalating attacks between Delaney and challenger David Trone highlight primary intensity but ensure a well-resourced Democratic standard-bearer.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli commands trader consensus at 38% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship after dominating the Miami Grand Prix with pole-to-win performance on May 4, securing his third victory and extending his points lead to 100-80 over Mercedes teammate George Russell. The 19-year-old rookie's flawless start—P2 in Australia, maiden win in China—highlights Mercedes' superior car pace and reliability, while Red Bull's Max Verstappen (6.7%, 26 points, seventh) and McLaren's Lando Norris (11.6%, 51 points, fourth) falter amid setup issues and consistency lapses. Russell's Australian GP triumph sustains his 29.5% standing, framing an intra-team rivalry as the title decider amid 21 races remaining.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former Lt. Gov. Mandela Barnes leads the Wisconsin Democratic gubernatorial primary at 52.5% implied probability on Polymarket, reflecting trader consensus on his superior statewide name recognition from prior campaigns as lieutenant governor and 2022 U.S. Senate nominee, positioning him as the frontrunner in a crowded field ahead of the August 11 primary. State Rep. Francesca Hong trails at 34%, buoyed by progressive grassroots momentum and early polling leads among decided Democratic voters in the February Marquette survey, though over 60% remained undecided. Recent prediction market surges for Barnes lack fresh polling or endorsement triggers post-April Supreme Court election, underscoring high uncertainty with Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez and Milwaukee County Exec. David Crowley further back amid potential shifts from debates or fundraising reports.

eventMetadata.context_description: Celtic's commanding 79% implied probability stems from their Scottish Premiership pedigree and recent 3-1 Old Firm victory over Rangers on May 10, extending a strong run including wins over Hibernian and Falkirk amid a tight title race where they sit second behind Hearts. Facing Championship fourth-placers Dunfermline at neutral Hampden Park in the Scottish Cup final, traders see vast quality gap despite the Pars' giantkilling path to the decider via triumphs over higher-tier sides like Hibs. Dunfermline's 20.5% upset chance and 21% draw reflect potential from returning striker Zak Rudden and defender Freddie Turley, while Celtic manages absences like Kasper Schmeichel's shoulder issue and Tomas Cvancara's recent groin blow with squad depth.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee has maintained the federal funds rate target range at 3.50%-3.75% for three consecutive meetings, most recently on April 28-29, 2026, amid sticky inflation and a resilient labor market. March CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year, exceeding February's 2.4%, while April nonfarm payrolls added 115,000 jobs—below expectations but with unemployment steady at 4.3%. The March dot plot median projects a single 25-basis-point cut by end-2026 to 3.25%-3.50%, with low-3% by 2027. CME Fed funds futures imply limited near-term easing, with trader consensus pricing gradual declines before 2027 conditional on softer inflation. Key catalysts include today's April CPI release (forecast +3.7% YoY) and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 4°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Met Office observational data confirms London's overnight low reached 5°C around 23:00 UTC on May 11 into early May 12, 2026, with temperatures quickly rising to 7–10°C by morning amid brisk northwesterly winds and increasing cloud cover—locking in the daily minimum per official records likely used for resolution, such as Heathrow Airport measurements. This aligns with forecast model consensus from the latest update at 06:51 UTC, projecting a daytime high of 16–17°C and tonight's minimum at 8°C under partly cloudy skies, explaining the 99.5% market-implied probability on 5°C as trader consensus reflects skin-in-the-game confidence in the data. Realistic challenges include rare measurement revisions from colder rural stations or unexpected evening radiative cooling, though light winds and cloud reduce that risk; monitor hourly updates through midnight UTC for final confirmation.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects EUR/USD's recent climb to around 1.175, driven by Eurozone inflation surges tied to Iran-related geopolitical tensions, prompting markets to price an 86% chance of an ECB deposit rate hike to 2.25% at the June 10 meeting—up from the current 2.00%—while the Fed holds the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% post its April 28-29 decision, with cut expectations fading amid resilient U.S. data. This narrowing rate differential supports modest euro strength, though dollar resilience persists on U.S. growth outperformance. Key catalysts ahead include Eurozone CPI on May 30, U.S. NFP on June 6, and divergent FOMC/ECB June policy sessions, with year-end forecasts varying from 1.14 to 1.28 amid uncertainty.

Mallorca's Vedat Muriqi (21 goals, Pichichi contender) remains a props focal point after scoring in their 1-1 Villarreal draw, but Getafe's home defense and low-scoring H2H trends (three 1-goal margins in last five) temper expectations in this tight late-season encounter.

markets.question: US strike on Colombia by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a US-initiated drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Colombia is announced or credibly reported to have occurred by the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including FPV and ATGM strikes as well as cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by any United States operatives, including military forces, intelligence agencies, or other U.S. government operatives, that physically impact ground territory within the listed country.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory (including rivers, lakes, ports, but excluding territorial sea) of the listed country counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, ground incursions, naval shelling, or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Any strike occurring during this market’s timeframe that is claimed by either Donald Trump or the U.S. government will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

This market will remain open until the end of the second day after the resolution time. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by that time, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tensions between the Trump administration and Colombian President Gustavo Petro have fueled speculation, but trader consensus views a US drone, missile, or airstrike on Colombian soil as unlikely by January 31, given the distinction from ongoing US military actions against alleged narco-trafficking boats at sea. The most recent verifiable development—a US strike on May 5 in the eastern Pacific killing two suspects—adds to a campaign exceeding 180 deaths since late 2025, yet falls outside territorial boundaries per market criteria. Petro's January warnings of a "real threat" followed Trump's rhetoric post-Venezuela intervention, but February saw joint vows to target narco bosses. Colombia's May 31 presidential election, with center-right frontrunner Paloma Valencia favored, could further de-escalate via policy realignment on drugs and Venezuela.

markets.question: Will the Reserve Bank of New Zealand decrease the official cash rate after the May decision?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the official cash rate (OCR) resulting from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s May monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Reserve Bank of New Zealand after its May 27, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Reserve Bank of New Zealand monetary policy schedule: https://www.rbnz.govt.nz/news-and-events/events#sort=%40eventstart%20ascending&f:@hierarchicalz95xsz120xatopictagnames=[Monetary%20policy]

This market may resolve as soon as the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's media release for their May 27, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the official cash rate is issued by the date of the next scheduled monetary policy decision, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

series.title: Reserve Bank of New Zealand Decision

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 88% implied probability to no change in the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's Official Cash Rate (OCR) at its May 27, 2026 Monetary Policy Statement, reflecting balanced inflation and labor dynamics following the April 8 hold at 2.25%. March 2026 quarter CPI remained sticky at 3.1% annually—near the top of the 1-3% target band—prompting a brief uptick in hike odds to 13.5% amid elevated consumer inflation expectations noted in the May Financial Stability Report. However, subdued 2.0% annual wage growth and a resilient unemployment rate dipping to 5.3% in the same period have contained rate-rise pressures, rendering a decrease at 0.1% improbable absent sharper downturn signals.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's ninth studio album Iceman drops May 15 via OVO Sound, capping a year-long teaser campaign highlighted by a $1.2 million Toronto ice sculpture stunt that revealed the date. Trader sentiment centers on official Spotify transcripts for mentions of key terms, with high consensus on introspective nods to fatherhood, pandemic reflections, and hometown Raptors pride amid Drake's post-Kendrick Lamar beef pivot toward manosphere themes. Fresh fuel arrived May 11 when Lamar deleted and re-uploaded diss track "Not Like Us," briefly spiking beef-related bets despite leaks hinting indirect shots at DJ Khaled and A$AP Rocky. Episode 4 livestream hits May 14; post-release lyrics and streaming metrics will swiftly dictate resolutions and debut trajectory.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.
